name: Monuments
description: A structures expansion for Twilight Imperium 4th edition, including Prophecy of Kings, Thunder's Edge, Discordant Stars and Blue Reveries expansions.
overview:
The **Monuments+** Expansion is a stand-alone mini-expansion of cards marked with the original Monuments mini-expansion logo and a +. It can be used with the base *Twilight Imperium: Fourth Edition* game as well as the *Prophecy of Kings* and *Thunder’s Edge* expansions. It extends the base *Monuments* mini-expansion designed by Blarknob, and contains assets to support factions in the fan expansions *Discordant Stars*, *Blue Reverie*, and related content.

At its core, this mini-expansion aims to encourage a variant strategic option for supported Twilight Imperium factions by placing their faction monument unit. The intent is to lean on faction strengths without simply adding a ‘more of the same’ ability (eg. more infantry for Sol, more technology for Jol-Nar, etc.), and to emphasize the importance of strategic placement. These units are not purely intended to rebalance or strengthen/nerf any particular factions.

blocks.content:
##Last updated: 2026-7-7 DRAFT

You can introduce Monuments easily using the included 'Cultural Monuments' event.
The Thunder's Edge Monuments event has been Omegad in this expansion to avoid terminology conflicts.
The cards by faction groups for official Twilight Imperium expansions.
The cards by faction groups for fan expansion Twilight Imperium content.
This section lists all other regular game component cards printed to go with this expansion, as well as some reminder cards to facilitate gameplay with this mini-expansion.

Some monuments have effects linked to the planet they are on when you exhaust the planet - these, as well as additional faction cards like Last Bastion's station, are included as well, to be placed under the planets in your planet area as a reminder.
Generic reminder cards per faction are included as simple placement rules cards, or cards to place under a planet with your monument in case that's important to you.
Additional tokens are included in lieu of unit models, and for relevant reminder effects of faction monuments.
This is a full replacement "Construction" strategy card for those so inclined.

**Q: What happens when I Nekro “M” the Spire of Ixth?**
A: The copy benefits the Mahact player only (this is deliberate, to avoid ‘foreign token scripts in RAM’ buffer overflow errors). Sorry Nekro.

**Q: Does The Panopticon just go anywhere on the map (excluding the Fracture), as long as there is no planet or anomaly there?**
A: Yep. If you (not-Empyrean) want to stop it spawning Empyrean units, next turn, you should blockade and destroy it ASAP.

**Q: Can I redeploy a monument with its deploy ability?**
A: No, deploy (as per LRR 30) is only possible from your reinforcements.

**Q: Can I place a monument twice using the new Construction primary?**
A: Yep. Most of the time this won’t be useful, but the N’orr monument is deliberately designed to benefit from that choice.

**Q: Hey wait The Obsidian monument has no placement text; is that a typo?**
A: Nope, once Obsidian commits to the relic’s whispers that monument is no longer valid to place on any planet. Flip wisely.

The following design guidelines are highly recommended for any home brewers crafting their own monument (or alternate
monuments) for their own factions or homebrew shenanigans.

Monuments should always care where they are located. Making them easily defended by a player because they are crammed near (or even on) a home system means they just become an additional power, not an additional option, and that’s what Breakthroughs are for. You’ll note most monuments refer only to adjacent systems, or “this” system, or units in adjacent systems. This means that the choice to place them by a player is meaningful.

A monument’s placement limitation is a strong gate on its powers. If you limit something to Mecatol Rex or a legendary planet, then map randomness may significantly limits its usefulness.

Monuments ideally provide an additional strategy for a faction rather than a simple buff to its existing powers. This isn’t a hard-and-fast rule, since Sol, Argent, N’Orr ignore it a little.

Monuments are a strong incentive to take Construction (a quick way to catch up for awful construction objectives). This means that a faction that wants to take construction naturally (like Vuil’Raith or even Titans) doesn’t need an additional incentive to take it. Note therefore that eg. The Vuil’Raith monument is designed to be worse when you don’t use its Deploy ability.

Most monuments shouldn’t do anything if they’re not on the board. It’s unintuitive and confusing.
However, a few factions, such as Winnu and Keleres and Nekro (stupid Nekro) interact with the game as cards too, instead of only units. The card should tell you what it does, so just read the cards as written.

Action Cards/Festival.jpg.description: ***ACTION:*** Ready a non-home planet that contains 1 of your structures. If that planet contains your monument, place 4 infantry of 1 mech on it.
Action Cards/Festival.jpg.title: Festival

Agendas/Cathedral of Ixth.jpg.description:
**Elect non-home planet**
Attach this card to the elected planet. The owner of this planet gains 1 VP if their monument is on this planet.
When a player places their monument on this planet, they gain 1 VP. When a player's monument on this planet is removed, they lose 1 VP.

faq.a: No, you only lose the point according to the law's rules. If the law was removed before you lost your monument, that point is locked in.
faq.q: If I remove this law with an action card, do I lose the VP if I got it when the law resolved?

Agendas/Cathedral of Ixth.jpg.subtitle: Law
Agendas/Cathedral of Ixth.jpg.title: Cathedral of Ixth

Agendas/Minister of Culture.jpg.description:
**Elect player**
As long as the owner of this card controls their monument their commodity value is increased by 3.
The elected players gains this card and replenishes their commodities.
Agendas/Minister of Culture.jpg.subtitle: Law
Agendas/Minister of Culture.jpg.title: Minister of Culture

faq.a: This one is for people who want to somehow print their own replacement strategy card. I'm not one of those people but hey, you do you.
faq.q: I'm confused, what about the strategy mini card?

Monument Funding.jpg.description:
After you perform the primary ability of the "Construction" strategy card, if your monument is not on the game board, you may place your monument.
Players cannot place monuments using the secondary ability of the "Construction" strategy card.
Monument Funding.jpg.flavour:
Raise a monument.
Raze a monument.
Monument Funding.jpg.title: Monument Funding

Monuments/BR/Atokera.jpg.back: Backs/Faction Monument Backs/Blue Reverie/Atokera Alliance Back.jpg
Monuments/BR/Atokera.jpg.description: During the agenda phase, when an outcome you voted for or predicted is resolved, produce up to 1 unit in this system.
Monuments/BR/Atokera.jpg.flavour: The Deepmantle's spike drives the Atokera into a planet's heart.
Monuments/BR/Atokera.jpg.subtitle: Atokera Monument
Monuments/BR/Atokera.jpg.title: Deepmantle Interface

Monuments/BR/Belkosea.jpg.back: Backs/Faction Monument Backs/Blue Reverie/Belkosean Alliance Back.jpg
Monuments/BR/Belkosea.jpg.description: At the end of the strategy phase, choose and place 1 control token on a super weapon card in your play area. This card has the text abilities of each superweapon card in your play area that has 1 of your control tokens.
Monuments/BR/Belkosea.jpg.subtitle: Belkosea Monument
Monuments/BR/Belkosea.jpg.title: Armageddon Project

Monuments/BR/Kaltrim.jpg.back: Backs/Faction Monument Backs/Blue Reverie/Kaltrim Alliance Back.jpg
Monuments/BR/Kaltrim.jpg.description:
***DEPLOY:*** When the Kaltrim Crown token is revealed, place this structure on a planet in your home system.
While you have the Kaltrim Crown token, other players must spend.1 command token from their command sheet to activate this system.
Monuments/BR/Kaltrim.jpg.subtitle: Kaltrim Monument
Monuments/BR/Kaltrim.jpg.title: The Crown Temple

Monuments/BR/Pharad'n.jpg.back: Backs/Faction Monument Backs/Blue Reverie/Pharad'n Alliance Back.jpg
Monuments/BR/Pharad'n.jpg.description:
This unit is treated as a structure and a ground force.
***DEPLOY:*** During the "Commit Ground Forces" step, place this uni on a planet in a system adjacent to planet you control.
Monuments/BR/Pharad'n.jpg.title: The Black Pyramid

Monuments/BR/Qhet.jpg.back: Backs/Faction Monument Backs/Blue Reverie/Qhet Alliance Back.jpg
Monuments/BR/Qhet.jpg.description: When 1 or more of your infantry would be destroyed, you may exhaust this card to place those units on this planet instead.
Monuments/BR/Qhet.jpg.flavour: Even the singular Qhet corporates require a touchstone for space.
Monuments/BR/Qhet.jpg.subtitle: Qhet Monument
Monuments/BR/Qhet.jpg.title: Sacred Pools

Monuments/BR/Sarcosa.jpg.back: Backs/Faction Monument Backs/Blue Reverie/Sarcosa Alliance Back.jpg
Monuments/BR/Sarcosa.jpg.description: You may place this structure into coexistence on a non-home, non-Fracture planet adjacent to a system that contains your or neutral units.
Monuments/BR/Sarcosa.jpg.subtitle: Sarcosa Monument
Monuments/BR/Sarcosa.jpg.title: Raider Stronghold

Monuments/BR/Toldar - Dishonor.jpg.description:
If you have more Honor than Dishonor, flip this card.
After a player wins a combat in this system., they may draw a secret objective.
While your Dishonor is 8, this unit cannot be destroyed.
Monuments/BR/Toldar - Dishonor.jpg.subtitle: Toldar Monument
Monuments/BR/Toldar - Dishonor.jpg.title: Charnel Fane

Monuments/BR/Toldar - Honor.jpg.description:
If you have more Dishonour and Honour, flip this card.
This unit gains the abilities matching your Honor:
2+: **PRODUCTION 2**
5+: **PLANETARY SHIELD**
8: **SPACE CANNON 4 (x3)**
Monuments/BR/Toldar - Honor.jpg.subtitle: Toldar Monument
Monuments/BR/Toldar - Honor.jpg.title: The People's Temple

Monuments/BR/Uydai.jpg.back: Backs/Faction Monument Backs/Blue Reverie/Uydai Alliance Back.jpg
Monuments/BR/Uydai.jpg.description: At the start of your turn, if this system contains 1 of your command tokens, you may exhaust this card to swap a plan card on your faction sheet for any other plan card.
Monuments/BR/Uydai.jpg.subtitle: Uydai Monument
Monuments/BR/Uydai.jpg.title: Hidden Oasis

Monuments/BR/Xin.jpg.back: Backs/Faction Monument Backs/Blue Reverie/Xin Alliance Back.jpg
Monuments/BR/Xin.jpg.description:
When a player activates this system, gain 1 balance token or flip 1 balance token on your faction sheet.
This unit may also be placed on a legendary planet.
Monuments/BR/Xin.jpg.subtitle: Xin Monument
Monuments/BR/Xin.jpg.title: Celestial Court

Monuments/Base+PoK/Arborec.jpg.description: When you pass, place 1 infantry on a planet in or adjacent to this system other than Mecatol Rex. If that planet contains another player's units, place that unit into coexistence.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Arborec.jpg.subtitle: Arborec Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Arborec.jpg.title: Flaah Orbitals

Monuments/Base+PoK/Argent.jpg.description:
***ACTION:*** Exhaust this card to choose this or an adjacent system that does not contain 1 or more of your ships.
Produce a hit against each ship in that system that has **SUSTAIN DAMAGE**.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Argent.jpg.subtitle: Argent Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Argent.jpg.title: Phoenix Cauldron

Monuments/Base+PoK/Creuss.jpg.description:
**After a player activates this system:**
You may produce 1 hit against players' ships in each system adjacent to this planet that contains a wormhole.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Creuss.jpg.subtitle: Creuss Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Creuss.jpg.title: Revenance Circuit

Monuments/Base+PoK/Empyrean.jpg.description:
This unit is placed in the space area of any non-Fracture, non-supernova system that does not contain planets, instead of on a planet. 
If this unit is blockaded, destroy it.

faq.a: Sure, if you can defend it.
faq.q: Wait can I snipe a Scar with this?

Monuments/Base+PoK/Empyrean.jpg.subtitle: Empyrean Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Empyrean.jpg.title: The Panopticon

Monuments/Base+PoK/Hacan.jpg.description: Outside of combat, players may trade their units in the space area of this and adjacent systems as part of a transaction. A player who gained units this way then replaces them and moves them to a system that contains their ships and 1 of their command tokens, ia able; otherwise, remove those units.

faq.a: It's a way for players to sell (and move and transport) units near your Freeport. Buy a fleet to go wage war on the other side of a galaxy! Maybe buy a fleet just to get rid of it.
faq.q: This is really complicated, what is happening?

Monuments/Base+PoK/Hacan.jpg.subtitle: Hacan Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Hacan.jpg.title: Mowshir Freeport

Monuments/Base+PoK/Jol-Nar.jpg.description:
**After an agenda outcome you voted for or predicted is resolved:**
Place up to 2 infantry on a planet you control in this system.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Jol-Nar.jpg.flavour: The sussurus of the falls calms even the most traumatised troops.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Jol-Nar.jpg.subtitle: Jol-Nar Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Jol-Nar.jpg.title: Calm Seas Sanatorium

Monuments/Base+PoK/L1Z1X.jpg.description: At the start of your turn, you may exhaust this card to take a player's command token from an adjacent system and place it in this system.
Monuments/Base+PoK/L1Z1X.jpg.flavour: This critical L1 node filters and redirects network traffic.
Monuments/Base+PoK/L1Z1X.jpg.subtitle: L1Z1X Mindnet

Monuments/Base+PoK/Letnev.jpg.description:
Double the **PRODUCTION** values of your units in this system,
When you exhaust this planet to cast votes, you may cast an additional number of votes equal to the number of your non-fighter ships in this system.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Letnev.jpg.subtitle: Lenten Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Letnev.jpg.title: Letnev

Monuments/Base+PoK/Mentak.jpg.description: At the start of a player’s turn, exhaust this card to have each ship in this or an adjacent system with **SUSTAIN DAMAGE** become damaged.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Mentak.jpg.flavour: Whether Erwan's Fist is a weapon or team of infiltrators is unknown.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Mentak.jpg.subtitle: Mental Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Mentak.jpg.title: Erwan's Fist

Monuments/Base+PoK/Muaat.jpg.description: When you exhaust this planet to cast votes, for each war sun or flagship you control in this system, and for each supernova adjacent to this system, choose: cast 3 additional votes, or produce a hit against another player's ships in this or an adjacent system.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Muaat.jpg.subtitle: Muuat Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Muaat.jpg.title: Glory Furnace

Monuments/Base+PoK/N'Orr.jpg.description:
When you place your monument, produce up to a total of 3 infantry or fighters in this or adjacent system without spending resources.
At the end of your turn, you may replace this unit with a different structure from your reinforcements.
Monuments/Base+PoK/N'Orr.jpg.subtitle: N'orr Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/N'Orr.jpg.title: G'hom Firefly Beacon

Monuments/Base+PoK/NRA.jpg.description: When you are negotiating a transaction with a player who controls units in or adjacent to this system, relics can be exchanged as part of that transaction; ignore 'when you gain' effects of those relics.
Monuments/Base+PoK/NRA.jpg.flavour: This vast hall of art and relics hides a more practical purpose.
Monuments/Base+PoK/NRA.jpg.subtitle: Naaz-Rokha Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/NRA.jpg.title: Quantum Reliquary

Monuments/Base+PoK/Naalu.jpg.description: Infantry that you commit to planets in this and adjacent systems may be committed into coexistence. if able.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Naalu.jpg.flavour: Reports from nearby systems confirm that all citizens are calm.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Naalu.jpg.subtitle: Naalu Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Naalu.jpg.title: Nefishh Projector

Monuments/Base+PoK/Nekro.jpg.description:
When you destroy another player's structure, you may place or move an "M" or "Z" assimilator token onto that player's monument card.
While any of those tokens are on a monument card, this card gains that card's attributes, abilities, and ability text.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Nekro.jpg.subtitle: Nekro Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Nekro.jpg.title: Valefar Assimilator "M"

Monuments/Base+PoK/Nomad.jpg.description:
**At the start of your turn:**
You may return this unit to your reinforcements to produce, or remove and produce, your flagship in this system.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Nomad.jpg.flavour: Reports of strange visions of this ornate shrine have been dismissed.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Nomad.jpg.subtitle: Nomad Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Nomad.jpg.title: Lodestar

Monuments/Base+PoK/Saar.jpg.description: You may treat this system as being adjacent to 1 of your space docks.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Saar.jpg.flavour: A monument to the Call, glowing in the dark.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Saar.jpg.subtitle: Saar Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Saar.jpg.title: Beacon of Ragh

Monuments/Base+PoK/Sol.jpg.description: When you place 1 or more infantry on a planet, exhaust this card to place any of those infantry on this planet instead.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Sol.jpg.flavour: The Cenotaph inevitably provides motivation for tired Sol troops.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Sol.jpg.subtitle: Sol Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Sol.jpg.title: Sol

Monuments/Base+PoK/Titans.jpg.description: **ACTION:*** Exhaust this card to place a sleeper token on a planet in this or an adjacent system that has a planetary trait.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Titans.jpg.flavour: The towering sceptre is both tool and rallying cry.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Titans.jpg.subtitle: Ul Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Titans.jpg.title: Scepter of Ul

Monuments/Base+PoK/Vuil'Raith.jpg.description: **DEPLOY**: When you gain control of a planet that contains 1 or more structures, you may place this unit on that planet and capture each other structure on that planet as if it were a non-fighter ship.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Vuil'Raith.jpg.subtitle: Vuil'Raith Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Vuil'Raith.jpg.title: She Consumes Worlds

Monuments/Base+PoK/Winnu.jpg.description: At the start of the agenda phase, if your monument is on the game board, gain any number of trade goods on this card. Otherwise, spend any number of resources to place 1 trade good from the supply on this card for every 3 resources spent.

faq.a: Yes, you can save money on the vault safely before you crack it open.
faq.q: Does this work before I place my monument?

Monuments/Base+PoK/Winnu.jpg.subtitle: Winnu Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Winnu.jpg.title: The Imperial Vault

Monuments/Base+PoK/Xxcha.jpg.description: When an agenda outcome you voted for or predicted is resolved, you may exhaust this card to place a structure on a planet you control in this system.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Xxcha.jpg.flavour: The array is an Xxcha infrastructure touchstone, and well defended.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Xxcha.jpg.subtitle: Xxcha Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Xxcha.jpg.title: Qanoj Shield Array

Monuments/Base+PoK/Yin.jpg.description: ***ACTION:*** Exhaust this card. Then, for every 2 infantry on this planet, move 1 of those infant to any other planet you control.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Yin.jpg.flavour: The sanctum is a site of pilgrimage that focuses Yin fervour.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Yin.jpg.subtitle: Yin Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Yin.jpg.title: The Blessed Sanctum

Monuments/Base+PoK/Yssaril.jpg.description:
**When another player plays an action card:**
If that player controls 1 or more units in this or an adjacent system, you may gain 2 trade goods.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Yssaril.jpg.flavour: The Yssaril keep a secret name for this place.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Yssaril.jpg.subtitle: Yssaril Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Yssaril.jpg.title: Sector KVD-14

Monuments/DS/Augurs.jpg.description:
When you would explore another planet, you may explore this planet instead.
If you do, you may gain trade goods equal to the number of your scored objectives.
Monuments/DS/Augurs.jpg.flavour: The technological edge provided by the planet was ... clarifying.
Monuments/DS/Augurs.jpg.subtitle: Augurs Monument
Monuments/DS/Augurs.jpg.title: AI Conclave

Monuments/DS/Bentor.jpg.description: **DEPLOY:** After another player explores a planet that is not adjacent to a home system, you may place this unit into coexistence on that planet and explore it.

faq.a: No, if you can't do all the ability you can't deploy.
faq.q: Can I DEPLOY this if there isn't anything to coexist with though? Like if another effect let the player explore their controlled, empty planet?

Monuments/DS/Bentor.jpg.subtitle: Bentor Monument
Monuments/DS/Bentor.jpg.title: Tucc Academy

Monuments/DS/Celdauri.jpg.description: During each of your tactical actions, you may commit ground forces on this planet to planets that contain your space docks.
Monuments/DS/Celdauri.jpg.flavour: An accident, turned into an asset.
Monuments/DS/Celdauri.jpg.subtitle: Celdauri Monument
Monuments/DS/Celdauri.jpg.title: Hyperlane Relay

Monuments/DS/Cheiran.jpg.description: The unit's **PRODUCTION** value is equal to the number of structures you control in or adjacent to this system.
Monuments/DS/Cheiran.jpg.subtitle: Cheiran Monument
Monuments/DS/Cheiran.jpg.title: Polemarch's Hive

Monuments/DS/Cymiae.jpg.description: When another player activates this system they must discard 1 action card, if able.
Monuments/DS/Cymiae.jpg.flavour: In the palace, the Voices are amplified, and Jatta's pain expands.
Monuments/DS/Cymiae.jpg.subtitle: Cymiae Monument
Monuments/DS/Cymiae.jpg.title: Jatta's Palace

Monuments/DS/Dih-Mohn.jpg.description: After you activate a system, you may exhaust this card to apply +1 to the move value of ships in this system until the end of this tactical action.
Monuments/DS/Dih-Mohn.jpg.flavour: The Cyclotron is the glowing apex of Flotilla engineering.
Monuments/DS/Dih-Mohn.jpg.subtitle: Dih-Mohn Monument
Monuments/DS/Dih-Mohn.jpg.title: Flotilla Cyclotron

Monuments/DS/Edyn.jpg.description: At any time, you may exhaust this card to produce a mech on this planet without spending resources.
Monuments/DS/Edyn.jpg.flavour: The throne is raised only when the Edyn feel the time is right.
Monuments/DS/Edyn.jpg.subtitle: Edyn Monument
Monuments/DS/Edyn.jpg.title: Twilight Throne

Monuments/DS/FSC.jpg.description: After you resolve the primary or secondary ability of the "Leadership" strategy card, each other player with units in or adjacent to this system who reveals 1 of your promissory notes from their hand gains 1 command token.
Monuments/DS/FSC.jpg.subtitle: Free Systems Monument
Monuments/DS/FSC.jpg.title: Independence Tower

Monuments/DS/Florzen.jpg.description:
When you produce fighters, you may place that many plastic fighter units on this card, instead.
At the start of your turn, you may produce any fighters on this card in this system without spending resources.
Monuments/DS/Florzen.jpg.subtitle: Florzen Monument
Monuments/DS/Florzen.jpg.title: Corsairs' Cove

Monuments/DS/GLEdge.jpg.description: At the start of your turn, you may exhaust 2 planets you control that contain a Core token; 1 player treats all laws as blank until the end of this turn.
Monuments/DS/GLEdge.jpg.flavour: "The GLEDge Verdant Halo Institute [GVHI] abides by all Galactic Council status that comply with §736 b. Lazax oversight."
Monuments/DS/GLEdge.jpg.title: Verdant Halo

Monuments/DS/Ghemina.jpg.description: You may treat this system as adjacent to systems that contain 2 of your structures of the same unit type.
Monuments/DS/Ghemina.jpg.subtitle: Ghemina Monument
Monuments/DS/Ghemina.jpg.title: Halls of Valor

Monuments/DS/Ghoti.jpg.description:
During movement, you may choose to treat this system as if it contains no planets.
You treat this system as though it contains a frontier token.
Monuments/DS/Ghoti.jpg.flavour: What the anchor point represents is unknown, but the Ghoti revere it.
Monuments/DS/Ghoti.jpg.subtitle: Ghoti Monument
Monuments/DS/Ghoti.jpg.title: Ghoti Anchorpoint

Monuments/DS/Kjalengard.jpg.description: Once per action, when a player wins a combat in or adjacent to this system, you and that player may each gain 1 command token.
Monuments/DS/Kjalengard.jpg.flavour: This is the most renowned of all Kjalengard bannerhalls.
Monuments/DS/Kjalengard.jpg.subtitle: Kjalengard Monument
Monuments/DS/Kjalengard.jpg.title: Krotas Bannerhall

Monuments/DS/Kolume.jpg.description: When another player passes, choose this or an adjacent system that contains a unit with **SPACE CANNON**; either use this unit's **SPACE CANNON** against another player's ships in that system, or repeat this ability, starting in that system.

faq.a: You are thematically ricocheting from the monument to a target system that contains a unit with **SPACE CANNON**.
faq.q: Wait, this unit or that unit?

faq.a: No, other players put **SPACE CANNONS** in range of this monument at their own risk.
faq.q: Does the target system unit have to be mine?

Monuments/DS/Kolume.jpg.subtitle: Kolume Monument
Monuments/DS/Kolume.jpg.title: Wonell's Bastion

Monuments/DS/Kyro.jpg.description:
This planet counts as a legendary planet with an attachment and a technology specialty of no type.
When this unit is destroyed, you must place it on a valid adjacent planet you control, if able. If you do, draw a relic.
Monuments/DS/Kyro.jpg.subtitle: Kyro Monument
Monuments/DS/Kyro.jpg.title: Kyro Reliquary

Monuments/DS/Lanefir.jpg.description: Once per action, you may use the technology speciality of a planet in on ardjacent to this system, even if it is exhausted.
Monuments/DS/Lanefir.jpg.flavour: The Library permanently displays a reminder of Lanefir loss.
Monuments/DS/Lanefir.jpg.subtitle: Lanefir Monument
Monuments/DS/Lanefir.jpg.title: Forbidden Library

Monuments/DS/Li-Zho.jpg.description: When a player activates this system, remove 1 command token from this system or gain 1 command token.
Monuments/DS/Li-Zho.jpg.flavour: Nightfall Fortress is an endless maze of traps: physical and moral.
Monuments/DS/Li-Zho.jpg.subtitle: Li-Zho Monument
Monuments/DS/Li-Zho.jpg.title: Nightfall Fortress

Monuments/DS/Mortheus.jpg.description: ***ACTION:*** Exhaust this card and choose an adjacent system that contains another player's non-fighter ships. That player moves those ships into this system if able, until they have moved at least 4 resources worth of ships, or all ships. Then, resolve a space combat.
Monuments/DS/Mortheus.jpg.subtitle: Mortheus Monument
Monuments/DS/Mortheus.jpg.title: Mirrorfore

Monuments/DS/Nokar.jpg.description: Your ships may move out of this system, and transport units, even if it contains 1 of your command tokens.
Monuments/DS/Nokar.jpg.flavour: Freehold by name, though nothing is truly free among the Nokar.
Monuments/DS/Nokar.jpg.subtitle: Nokar Monument
Monuments/DS/Nokar.jpg.title: Freehold Starport

Monuments/DS/Olradin.jpg.description: After you resolve the primary or secondary ability of the"Diplomacy" strategy card, ready this planet.
Monuments/DS/Olradin.jpg.flavour: The Olradin are masters of repurposing abandoned structures,
Monuments/DS/Olradin.jpg.subtitle: Olradin Monument
Monuments/DS/Olradin.jpg.title: Diplomatic Enclave

Monuments/DS/Rhodun Back.jpg.description:
Flip this at any time.
Other players with units in the Fracture cannot activate this system.
During combat, apply +2 to each of your units' combat rolls in this and adjacent systems if your opponent has any units in the Fracture.
Monuments/DS/Rhodun Back.jpg.subtitle: Rhodun Monument
Monuments/DS/Rhodun Back.jpg.title: Reliquat Unleashed

Monuments/DS/Rhodun Front.jpg.description:
Start with this side face-up; when a player activates this system, you may flip this card.
During combat, apply +1 to each of your units' combat rolls in this and adjacent systems if your opponent has any unit technology upgrades.
Monuments/DS/Rhodun Front.jpg.subtitle: Rhodun Monument
Monuments/DS/Rhodun Front.jpg.title: Vault of New Phrad

Monuments/DS/Tnelis.jpg.description:
When you lose a ground combat on this planet, you may destroy each unit on this planet.
At the start of your turn you may destroy 1 other player's coexisting unit in this system.
Monuments/DS/Tnelis.jpg.subtitle: Tnelis Monument
Monuments/DS/Tnelis.jpg.title: Dawnstar HQ

Monuments/DS/Vaden.jpg.description: After another player with ships in this or an adjacent system replenishes commodities, if that player has control tokens on the Vaden player's faction sheet, you may take one of their commodities.
Monuments/DS/Vaden.jpg.subtitle: Vaden Monument
Monuments/DS/Vaden.jpg.title: Bounty Brokerage

Monuments/DS/Zelian.jpg.description:
This system is treated as an asteroid field. Units can move into this system as it it were a non-anomaly tile.
Place the Zelian asteroid field token in this system as a reminder.
Monuments/DS/Zelian.jpg.subtitle: Zelian Monument
Monuments/DS/Zelian.jpg.title: Debris Field

Monuments/Other/Drahn.jpg.description: At the beginning of your turn you may remove this unit from this planet and place it onto a different planet you control.
Monuments/Other/Drahn.jpg.flavour: It is more a reminder of Drahn influence than a true 'monument'.
Monuments/Other/Drahn.jpg.subtitle: Drahn Monument
Monuments/Other/Drahn.jpg.title: Koldan's Outpost

Monuments/TE/Bastion.jpg.description:
The unit is a station and placed in the space area of a system that contains your ships and exactly 1 non-legendary planet. Take its matching station card while you control it.
When you lose control of this station, destroy it and produce 2 hits against ships in this system.
Monuments/TE/Bastion.jpg.subtitle: Bastion Monument
Monuments/TE/Bastion.jpg.title: Seraph Data Center

Monuments/TE/Crimson Rebellion.jpg.description: After a breach becomes or is place active side up in any system, if that system is adjacent to this planet, you may produce 1 hit in that system against another player's ships.

faq.q: Hey wait if there is an active breach in the monument system, then it's adjacent to the breach system right?

Monuments/TE/Crimson Rebellion.jpg.subtitle: Rebellion Monument
Monuments/TE/Crimson Rebellion.jpg.title: Veils of Lost Creuss

Monuments/TE/Deepwrought.jpg.description: Other players' ships in this system do not trigger space combat. Afer this unit is removed from a planet, choose a player with ships in that system to resolve a space combat. Repeat this until it contains no more than a player's ships.

faq.a: Look, it's functionally coexistence in space that you can't break, with an automatic clean up step.

Monuments/TE/Deepwrought.jpg.subtitle: Deepwrought Monument
Monuments/TE/Deepwrought.jpg.title: The Pelagion

Monuments/TE/Firmament.jpg.description: After a player activates this system, you may replace 1 control token on one of your plots with 1 of that player's control tokens.
Monuments/TE/Firmament.jpg.flavour: Epiphany offers a meeting place for the discussion of grand strategies,
Monuments/TE/Firmament.jpg.subtitle: Firmament Monument
Monuments/TE/Firmament.jpg.title: Epiphany

Monuments/TE/Keleres.jpg.description: After a law enters play you may add a command token from your reinforcements to this card. When you would place a command in this or an adjacent system, you may place that token from this card instead.

faq.a: Yes, only placing them requires a 'this' anchor.
faq.q: Can I add command tokens when the unit isn't on the board?

Monuments/TE/Keleres.jpg.subtitle: Keleres Monument
Monuments/TE/Keleres.jpg.title: Paris Iustitiae

Monuments/TE/Obsidian.jpg.description: After a puppeted player activates this system, replace up to 2 of that player's infantry on a planet they control with your infantry; those units coexist.

faq.a: No, there are no valid placements for this monument. You need to place it before it no longer has valid placements.
faq.q: Can I place this on a planet once it's flipped?

Monuments/TE/Obsidian.jpg.flavour: An unknown, twisted connection exists between visitors of this place.
Monuments/TE/Obsidian.jpg.subtitle: Decayed Monument
Monuments/TE/Obsidian.jpg.title: Epiphany Hollow

Monuments/TE/Ral-Nel.jpg.description: At the end of your tactical action, you may move 1 structure in the space area of this or an adjacent system to the space area of a system adjacent to that structure.
Monuments/TE/Ral-Nel.jpg.title: Sor Tek Linkhub

Monuments/TF/King Black.jpg.description:
Capture all infantry destroyed in this system.
You may spend captured infantry as if they were resources when producing units on this planet.
Monuments/TF/King Black.jpg.subtitle: Raithborn Monument
Monuments/TF/King Black.jpg.title: The Flesh Cathedral

Monuments/TF/King Blue.jpg.description:
At the start of any tactical action, you may choose any number of destroyers or cruisers in this system.
Those units without a capacity gain a capacity value of 1 until the end of this tactical action.
Monuments/TF/King Blue.jpg.subtitle: Tempered Monument
Monuments/TF/King Blue.jpg.title: Caracas

Monuments/TF/King Green.jpg.description:
At the start of a ground combat not involving your units in this and adjacent systems, you may produce 1 hit against a player's units in that combat.
Units cannot be placed into coexistence in this system.
Monuments/TF/King Green.jpg.subtitle: Blossoming Monument
Monuments/TF/King Green.jpg.title: The Crown of Thorns

Monuments/TF/King Orange.jpg.description: At the start of an action you may allow any players' mechs in this and adjacent systems to gain +1 to their combat rolls until the end that action.
Monuments/TF/King Orange.jpg.subtitle: Divine Monument
Monuments/TF/King Orange.jpg.title: The Starlit Redoubt

Monuments/TF/King Pink.jpg.description:
This unit is a ground force as well as a monument. It can be transported.
This unit gains the non-DEPLOY unit abilities and text abilities of your ground forces.
Monuments/TF/King Pink.jpg.subtitle: Replicant Monument
Monuments/TF/King Pink.jpg.title: Avatar of Mirrors

Monuments/TF/King Purple.jpg.description: This unit is placed in the space area of a system that contains a planet you control, instead of on a planet. 1 ship in this system does not count against your fleet limit.
Monuments/TF/King Purple.jpg.subtitle: Paradox Monument
Monuments/TF/King Purple.jpg.title: Halo Cortex

Monuments/TF/King Red.jpg.description: ***DEPLOY:*** At the start of a ground combat involving your units, place this unit on that planet, then produce 2 hits against each other player's units on this planet.

faq.a: No, 2 hits per player, to their units.

Monuments/TF/King Red.jpg.subtitle: Vermillion Monument
Monuments/TF/King Red.jpg.title: The Serrated Throne

Monuments/TF/King White.jpg.description: When you draw a relic, gain 1 trade good and produce 1 unit on this planet.
Monuments/TF/King White.jpg.subtitle: Arcane Monument
Monuments/TF/King White.jpg.title: The Alabaster Edifice

Monuments/TF/King Yellow.jpg.description:
When you splice, you may place 1 command token from your reinforcements on this card, if able.
You may remove a token from this card to cancel a hit in this or an adjacent system.
Monuments/TF/King Yellow.jpg.subtitle: Glimmering Monument
Monuments/TF/King Yellow.jpg.title: Aura Vault

Objectives/Topple Monument.jpg.description: Destroy another player's monument during a tactical action.
Objectives/Topple Monument.jpg.title: Topple Monument

Reminders/Edyn Monument.jpg.back: Backs/Faction Monument Backs/Vertical Backs/Edyn Alliance Vertical.jpg
Reminders/Edyn Monument.jpg.title: Twilight Throne

Reminders/Letnev Monument.jpg.back: Backs/Faction Monument Backs/Vertical Backs/Letnev Alliance Vertical.jpg
Reminders/Letnev Monument.jpg.title: Palatine Obelisk

Reminders/Muaat Monument.jpg.back: Backs/Faction Monument Backs/Vertical Backs/Muaat Alliance Vertical.jpg
Reminders/Muaat Monument.jpg.title: Glory Furnace
